pathway.title: Oral ingestion
pathway.description: Tablet or liquid enters the GI tract and dissolves for intestinal uptake.
pathway.importance: Defines oral bioavailability assumptions in the demo PK model.

pathway.title: Small-intestine absorption
pathway.description: Rapid absorption into portal circulation with moderate first-pass handling.
pathway.importance: Sets the rate constant feeding the plasma concentration curve.

pathway.title: Hepatic conjugation
pathway.description: Primarily glucuronidation and sulfation to inactive conjugates; high dose shifts toward oxidative pathways.
pathway.importance: Explains dose-dependent liver risk in screening narratives.

pathway.title: Systemic analgesia / antipyretic effect
pathway.description: Central effects reduce fever and pain; exact mechanism is simplified for the hackathon storyline.
pathway.importance: Connects PK exposure to the therapeutic story without claiming a single validated target.

pathway.title: Renal clearance
pathway.description: Conjugates are cleared in urine; renal impairment can prolong exposure in real patients.
pathway.importance: Links elimination to the falling tail of the simulated curve.

pathway.title: Overdose liability
pathway.description: Supratherapeutic doses increase oxidative metabolite burden — flagged as hepatotoxicity risk in the UI.
pathway.importance: Motivates the safety score and risk tiles for judges.

organ_notes.brain: Limited classical NSAID-style COX inhibition; central effects dominate the simplified narrative.
organ_notes.lungs: Low pulmonary retention in this heuristic map.
organ_notes.heart: Low direct cardiac signal in the MVP tox proxy.
organ_notes.liver: Primary metabolic hub; dose-dependent stress is the headline liability.
organ_notes.kidneys: Conjugate excretion dominates; dehydration and chronic use raise real-world risk (not fully modeled).
organ_notes.bloodstream: Peak concentration drives the chart; protein binding is moderate in the heuristic.

pathway.title: Oral ingestion
pathway.description: Acidic NSAID can irritate gastric mucosa — GI risk is highlighted in the risk list.
pathway.importance: Sets up COX-1–related GI warnings in the demo.

pathway.title: Rapid absorption
pathway.description: High oral bioavailability with quick rise to peak plasma levels.
pathway.importance: Shapes the early ascending limb of the PK curve.

pathway.title: Plasma protein binding
pathway.description: Very high binding limits free drug and CNS penetration in most patients.
pathway.importance: Feeds the protein-binding heuristic and distribution story.

pathway.title: COX inhibition
pathway.description: Blocks prostaglandin synthesis — anti-inflammatory and analgesic activity.
pathway.importance: Mechanistic label for the pathway explainer.

pathway.title: Hepatic metabolism
pathway.description: CYP-mediated oxidation and conjugation to polar metabolites.
pathway.importance: Explains clearance driving elimination from plasma.

pathway.title: Renal excretion
pathway.description: Metabolites cleared in urine; dehydration and CKD increase real nephrotoxicity risk.
pathway.importance: Supports nephrotoxicity monitoring flags in the UI.

organ_notes.brain: Peripheral action dominates due to high protein binding; CNS penetration is limited in the heuristic.
organ_notes.lungs: Asthma-sensitive pathways are not modeled in detail — outlier risk is qualitative only.
organ_notes.heart: Long-term CV risk is noted qualitatively in screening copy, not predicted quantitatively.
organ_notes.liver: Generally lower hepatotoxicity than acetaminophen in overdose narratives; still metabolized here.
organ_notes.kidneys: Hemodynamic NSAID effects are the main renal story for the hackathon dashboard.
organ_notes.bloodstream: High protein binding reduces free fraction in this toy model.

pathway.title: Oral absorption
pathway.description: Slow, variable absorption — exposure can accumulate with repeat dosing in real regimens.
pathway.importance: Justifies longer half-life and wider distribution in the demo.

pathway.title: Systemic distribution
pathway.description: Substantial tissue partitioning; teratogenic risk is the historical lesson for the UI.
pathway.importance: Drives a poor safety verdict independent of PK curve shape.

pathway.title: Placental crossing (warning)
pathway.description: Known human teratogen — used here as a cautionary education case, not a patient prediction.
pathway.importance: Explains why trial_ready should read false in the simulator.

pathway.title: Cereblon pathway (conceptual)
pathway.description: Modern biology implicates IMiD binding to cereblon in teratogenic and immunomodulatory effects — simplified label.
pathway.importance: Science communication for judges without over-claiming this MVP.

pathway.title: Metabolism
pathway.description: Multiple routes including spontaneous hydrolysis and enzymatic processing.
pathway.importance: Supports moderate liver share in the organ map.

pathway.title: Elimination
pathway.description: Renal clearance contributes; impaired renal function increases real-world accumulation.
pathway.importance: Completes ADME storytelling for the pathway card.

organ_notes.brain: Sedation and neuropathy risks are qualitative flags in historical labeling — not simulated as concentrations.
organ_notes.lungs: Thromboembolic risk in some regimens is noted in real labels; MVP uses generic risk language only.
organ_notes.heart: Thrombotic risk is a known clinical theme — surfaced as elevated cardiovigilance in the risk tiles.
organ_notes.liver: Less hepatocentric than acetaminophen overdose stories but still part of clearance.
organ_notes.kidneys: Excretion pathway matters for accumulation; heuristic kidney share is moderate.
organ_notes.bloodstream: Long half-life increases duration of systemic exposure in the simulated curve.

pathway.title: Oral ingestion
pathway.description: Direct gastric irritation possible; enteric coatings reduce but do not eliminate GI risk.
pathway.importance: Supports GI bleeding warnings in the risk assessment.

pathway.title: Rapid hydrolysis to salicylate
pathway.description: Ester hydrolysis yields salicylate — the main active moiety at steady exposure.
pathway.importance: Explains dose-dependent PK complexity (simplified to one compartment in MVP).

pathway.title: Irreversible COX acetylation
pathway.description: Long-lived platelet effects drive antithrombotic use and bleeding vigilance.
pathway.importance: Mechanistic hook for cardiovascular / bleeding risk tiles.

pathway.title: Hepatic conjugation
pathway.description: Glycine and glucuronide conjugation; saturable at high doses in reality.
pathway.importance: Links structure to nonlinear risk at overdose (qualitative only here).

pathway.title: Renal excretion
pathway.description: Salicylate clearance is pH-dependent in physiology — not parameterized in the toy model.
pathway.importance: Provides renal narrative for the organ explainer.

pathway.title: Pediatric caution
pathway.description: Reye syndrome risk with viral illness in children — educational warning in real labeling.
pathway.importance: Optional copy for the team pitch; surfaced as a qualitative risk theme.

organ_notes.brain: Salicylism at high doses (tinnitus) is a classic teaching point — not predicted numerically here.
organ_notes.lungs: Aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease is a known concern for susceptible asthma patients.
organ_notes.heart: Antiplatelet benefit at low dose vs bleeding risk at high dose — qualitative trade-off only.
organ_notes.liver: Therapeutic doses usually modest liver load; saturation appears at toxicologic exposures.
organ_notes.kidneys: Important clearance organ; interacts with GI blood loss risk in real patients.
organ_notes.bloodstream: Short effective half-life of parent aspirin vs longer salicylate — heavily simplified in charts.

pathway.title: Oral absorption
pathway.description: Incomplete but sufficient absorption; extended-release formulations alter the profile in practice.
pathway.importance: Moderate bioavailability in the PK heuristic.

pathway.title: Portal circulation
pathway.description: High hepatic exposure without classic CYP metabolism — biguanide pharmacology.
pathway.importance: Explains liver-centric glucose effects in teaching copy.

pathway.title: Mitochondrial / AMPK narrative
pathway.description: Simplified mention of reduced hepatic glucose output — not a mechanistic simulation.
pathway.importance: Connects drug class to the mechanism pathway card.

pathway.title: Minimal metabolism
pathway.description: Excreted largely unchanged — low metabolic liability in the tox heuristics.
pathway.importance: Supports favorable liver risk tiles for the demo.

pathway.title: Renal clearance
pathway.description: Major elimination route; contraindicated in significant renal impairment in real care.
pathway.importance: Elevates kidney monitoring in the risk list.

pathway.title: Lactic acidosis (rare)
pathway.description: Rare serious risk with renal or hypoxic stress — flagged qualitatively for education.
pathway.importance: Balances an otherwise favorable safety band.

organ_notes.brain: Limited CNS penetration; not a hypoglycemic driver as monotherapy in typical teaching models.
organ_notes.lungs: Acidosis risk under hypoxia is a teaching footnote — not simulated.
organ_notes.heart: Cardioprotective epidemiology is out of scope; no strong cardiac tox proxy in MVP.
organ_notes.liver: Pharmacologic target organ for glucose regulation with minimal intrinsic hepatotoxicity in the heuristic.
organ_notes.kidneys: Dominant clearance pathway — kidney share is high in the organ map.
organ_notes.bloodstream: Moderate distribution volume; renal clearance sets half-life in the toy PK.
